seoTitle: Centenary Heights vs Ceratodus — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Centenary Heights and Ceratodus. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Centenary Heights vs Ceratodus: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Centenary Heights and Ceratodus.
heading: Centenary Heights vs Ceratodus — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Centenary Heights, Queensland and Ceratodus, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Centenary Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Ceratodus (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Centenary Heights or Ceratodus safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Centenary Heights has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Ceratodus scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Centenary Heights scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Ceratodus scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Centenary Heights or Ceratodus?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Centenary Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
